title: IgG Kappa Multiple Myeloma with Cutaneous Plasmacytoma and Hypereosinophilic Leukocytosis in a Young Patient: A Rare Case Report.
abstract: Multiple myeloma (MM) is a malignant plasma cell disorder with a median age at diagnosis of 69 years. Its occurrence in individuals younger than 30 years is exceptionally uncommon, accounting for approximately 0.3% of all cases. Classic manifestations include bone pain, anemia, renal dysfunction, and hypercalcemia. Extramedullary disease (EMD), such as plasmacytomas, represents a more aggressive phenotype. Among these, cutaneous plasmacytomas-monoclonal plasma cell infiltrates in the skin-are exceedingly rare.
trial_name: Carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone for relapsed multiple myeloma
